    "text": "there are 4 bus routes from A to B and 3 routes from B to C. Therefore, there are\n4 × 3 = 12 ways to go from A to C. It is round trip so the man will travel back from C\nto A via B. It is restricted that man can not use same bus routes from C to B and B to\nA more than once. Thus, there are 2 × 3 = 6 routes for return journey. Therefore, the\nrequired number of ways = 12 × 6 = 72.\nExample 14  In how many ways a committee consisting of 3 men and 2 women, can\nbe chosen from 7 men and 5 women?\n(A)\n45\n(B)\n350\n(C)\n4200\n(D)\n230\nSolution  (B) is the correct choice. Out of 7 men, 3 men can be chosen in 7C3\nways and out of 5 women, 2 women can be chosen in 5C2 ways. Hence, the\ncommittee can be chosen in 7C3 × 5C2 = 350 ways.\nExample 15  All the letters of the word ‘EAMCOT’ are arranged in different possible\nways.     "text": "(A)\n69760\n(B)\n30240\n(C)\n99748\n(D)\n99784\nSolution  (A) is correct choice. Number of 5 letters words (with the condition that a\nletter can be repeated) = 105. Again number of words using 5 different letters is 10P5.\nTherefore, required number of letters\n= Total number of words – Total number of words in which no letter is repeated\n= 105 –  10P5 = 69760.\nExample 17  The number of signals that can be sent by 6 flags of different colours\ntaking one or more at a time is\n(A)\n63\n(B)\n1956\n(C)\n720\n(D)\n21\nSolution  The correct answer is B.\nNumber of signals using one flag = 6P1 = 6\nNumber of signals using two flags = 6P2 = 30\nNumber of signals using three flags = 6P3 = 120\nNumber of signals using four flags = 6P4 = 360\nNumber of signals using five flags = 6P5 = 720\nNumber of signals using all six flags = 6P6 = 720\nTherefore, the total number of signals using one or more flags at a time is\n6 + 30 + 120 + 360 + 720 + 720 = 1956 (Using addition principle).\nExample 18  In an examination there are three multiple choice questions and each\nquestion has 4 choices. Number of ways in which a student can fail to get all answer\ncorrect is\n(A)\n11\n(B)\n12\n(C)\n27\n(D)\n63\nSolution  The correct choice is (D). There are three multiple choice question, each\nhas four possible answers. Therefore, the total number of possible answers will be\n4 × 4 × 4 = 64. Out of these possible answer only one will be correct and hence the\nnumber of ways in which a student can fail to get correct answer is 64 – 1 = 63.\nExample 19  The straight lines l1, l2 and l3 are parallel and lie in the same plane. A\ntotal numbers of m points are taken on l1; n points on l2, k points on l3. The maximum\nnumber of triangles formed with vertices at these points are\n(A)\n(m + n + k)C3\n(B)\n(m + n + k)C3 – mC3 – nC3 – kC3\n(C)\nmC3 + nC3 + kC3\n(D)\nmC3 × nC3 × kC3\nSolution  (B) is the correct answer. Here the total number of points are (m + n + k)\nwhich must give (m + n + k)C3 number of triangles but m points on l1 taking 3 points at a\ntime gives mC3 combinations which  produce no triangle. Similarly, nC3 and kC3",
